rulings.Case Title: LI CHANG KUANG VS. KENNY WONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery; Points & Authorities; Declaration Of Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, July 1, 2026, line 1 2 - PLAINTIFF LI KUANG, TOMMY CHAN Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Plaintiffs Li Chang Kuang and Tommy Chan bring a motion to compel defendant Kenny Wong to respond to their form interrogatories, requests for admission, and special interrogatories. The motion is denied without prejudice. *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO COUNSEL* For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: CESAR AUGUSTO PORTELA BARRETO VS. GULSHAN RAI GOHRI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Memorandum Of Points And Authrorities In Support Of Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc.; Rasier, Llc, And Rasier-Ca, Llc'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, July 1, 2026, line 2, DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER, LLC, RASIER-CA, LLC'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc.; Rasier LLC, and Rasier-CA, LLC's motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted. The request for judicial notice is granted. Plaintiff Cesar Augusto Portela Barreto filed this personal injury action against the Uber defendants and the Uber driver, Gulshan Gohri, alleging negligence by the Gohri and vicarious liability by the Uber defendants. Plaintiff did not respond to Uber's discovery requests, and the court entered a deemed-admitted order as to Uber's request for admissions. The Uber defendants now move for judgment on the basis of the pleadings and the admissions. "On a motion for judgment on the pleadings, a trial court may rely upon facts established through party admissions." (Thomson v. Canyon (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 594, 602-603.) Considering plaintiffs' admissions and the complaint, the court concludes the Uber defendants are entitled to judgment. Plaintiff argues against this by contending that he did not receive the discovery and was unaware of it. However, a party has the responsibility of keeping the court and other parties updated about his address. The court discussed this with plaintiff at the prior hearing on Gohri's motion for judgment, and plaintiff has taken no steps to be relieved of the deemed-admitted order as to the Uber defendants. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ISABELLA KHENAISSER VS. KWOK YAM YAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Prohibiting His Deposition; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, July 1, 2026, line 8, DEFENDANT ANDREW COLLINS'Motion For Protective Order Prohibiting His Deposition Defendant and moving party Andrew Collins' motion for a protective order prohibiting his deposition is denied. Plaintiff Isabella Khenaisser's action asserts negligence, breach of contract, and breach of the implied warranty of habitability causes of action, among others, against a variety of defendants including Collins. Collins now seeks a protective order prohibiting him from being deposed on the grounds that he is the highest-ranking official of defendant Bungalow Living Inc., and the apex doctrine requires Khenaisser to show personal knowledge and exhaustion of other efforts before deposing him. But Collins is a defendant and has answered the complaint. (Answer, Sept. 17, 2025.) In addition, Khenaisser shows that Collins has personally responded to discovery. (E.g., Chiosso Decl., Ex. C.) None of the apex doctrine cases involved a corporate officer who was also a named defendant. Collins does not carry his burden of showing a protective order is proper. No party sought sanctions. The court gives notice to both parties that it will sua sponte consider sanctions against a party whose discovery position lacks substantial justification on future motions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (302/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: ALEXANDRA PICKOLICK VS. CAIHUA TANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order Prohibiting His Deposition; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, July 1, 2026, line 9, DEFENDANT ANDREW COLLINS' Motion For Protective Order Prohibiting His Deposition Defendant and moving party Andrew Collins' motion for a protective order prohibiting his deposition is denied. Plaintiff Alexandra Pickolick's action asserts negligence, breach of contract, and breach of the implied warranty of habitability causes of action, among others, against a variety of defendants including Collins. Collins now seeks a protective order prohibiting him from being deposed on the grounds that he is the highest-ranking official of defendant Bungalow Living Inc., and the apex doctrine requires Pickolick to show personal knowledge and exhaustion of other efforts before deposing him. But Collins is a defendant and has answered the complaint. (Answer, Sept. 17, 2025.) In addition, Pickolick shows that Collins has personally responded to discovery. (E.g., O'Brien Decl., Ex. B.) None of the apex doctrine cases involved a corporate officer who was also a named defendant. Collins does not carry his burden of showing a protective order is proper. No party sought sanctions. The court gives notice to both parties that it will sua sponte consider sanctions against a party whose discovery position lacks substantial justification on future motions. rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, July 1, 2026, line 11, DEFENDANT GORDON RAUSSER AN INDIVIDUAL, GORDON FALLONE, DAVID TEECE, MATTHEW DREDGE, SWYFT, INC.'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT Defendants Swyft, Inc., Gordon Rausser, Gordon Fallone, David Teece, and Matthew Dredge's demurrer to Plaintiffs Gower Smith, Lincoln Smith, Jordan Smith, and Microsmith Trust's complaint is overruled as to the first, second, fourth, and seventh causes of action. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the fifth cause of action for interference with contract.
